subparts.text: Discuss the properties of Bidirectional Reflection Distribution Function .
Explain how Bayesian formulation reflects the inverse relationship between computer vision and computer graphics.
Why is image preprocessing an essential step in computer vision applications?

A calibrated stereo camera system is being used to estimate the position of a point in a 3D scene. The camera has a focal length of 800 pixels along the x-axis. When observing a particular point, the disparity is measured to be 40 pixels, and the depth of this point from the camera system along the Z-axis is 4 meters. Calculate the baseline distance between the two cameras. Additionally, if the depth increases to 6 meters, what will happen to the disparity? Explain.
Consider a computer vision system in an autonomous vehicle. Describe how the system processes the visual information step by step, from analyzing the image to making a driving decision.
Derive an expression for the relationship between the radiance emitted or reflected from the scene and the irradiance measured at the camera sensor.
Write short notes on any two application of computer vision.
Explain the difference between continuous images and discrete images. Discuss the roles of sampling and quantization in this process.

Discuss the significance of gradient computation and non- maximum suppression in detecting edges. Suppose you apply the Sobel operator to a point in an image, and the gradient values in the x and y directions are Gx=3 and Gy=4. Calculate the magnitude and direction of the gradient at this point.

Explain the concept of optical flow in computer vision. How does it represent motion in a video sequence? In a video sequence, a small object moves across the image plane. At a particular pixel location the image intensity gradients are given by 𝜕𝐼 𝜕𝑦= 7, 𝜕𝐼 𝜕𝑥= 3 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝜕𝐼 𝜕𝑡= −1.Determine the relationship between velocity components of the pixel in the x and y directions at this point.
